name: Raised Mexican Cornbread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix yeast, cornmeal, and baking soda. Set aside.
2. In a saucepan, heat buttermilk, onion, oil, salt, and sugar until very warm (130°F).
3. Stir the warm mixture into the cornmeal mixture.
4. Beat in eggs, then cheese, corn, and peppers.
5. Add 6 cups of flour to the dough. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface.
6. Knead until smooth and elastic. Place in a greased bowl, turning once.
7. Cover and let rise until doubled (about 1-1/2 hours).
8. Grease two loaf pans with cornmeal dusting.
9. Divide the dough into two equal parts. Shape each into a loaf and place in the prepared pans.
10. Cover and let rise until doubled, about 30-45 minutes.
11. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
12. Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown.
13. Remove from pans and brush with melted margarine.
14. Let cool before serving.
